posting.sourceTitle: Plumbers and Pipefitters Binghamton LU #112 (Southern Zone)
posting.sponsorName: Plumbers and Pipefitters Binghamton Local Union #112
posting.locationSummary: Mohawk Valley and Southern Tier regions of New York
posting.applicationSummary: Apply online at ualocal112.org anytime during the recruitment period, or pick up and submit an application in person at Local Union #112 on the first working Monday of each month during regular business hours.
posting.applicationInPersonAddress: Local Union #112, 11 Griswold Street, Binghamton, NY 13904
posting.applicationHours: 8:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on the first working Monday of each month, excluding legal holidays, during the recruitment period.
posting.ageRequirement: Must be at least 18 years old at time of employment and sign an affidavit attesting to this.
posting.educationRequirement: Must have a high school diploma or high school equivalency diploma (such as TASC or GED), recognized by local school authorities, and sign an affidavit attesting to this.
posting.transportationRequirement: Must have reliable transportation to and from various job sites and required classes at the approved school.
posting.drugTestRequirement: Must pass a physical examination and a drug test after selection and prior to enrollment; the sponsor pays the cost.
posting.classRequirement: Must attend required classes at the approved school and sign a scholarship loan agreement in conjunction with classroom instruction classes if accepted to the program.
allRequirements: Must be at least 18 years old at time of employment and sign an affidavit attesting to this; proof may be required after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
Must have a high school diploma or a high school equivalency diploma (such as TASC or GED), as recognized by local school authorities, and sign an affidavit attesting to this; proof may be required.
Must pass a physical examination and a drug test, at the expense of the sponsor, after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.

Must sign an affidavit stating that they are physically able to do the work of a Steamfitter, which may include working regularly at varying heights and in hot and cold temperatures indoors and outdoors.
Must have reliable transportation to and from various job sites and required classes at the approved school.
Must provide DD-214, Certificate of Release or Discharge from Active Duty, if applicable, after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
Must sign a scholarship loan agreement in conjunction with classroom instruction classes, if accepted to the program.
posting.requirementsSummary: Applicants must be at least 18, have a high school diploma or equivalency, pass a sponsor-paid physical and drug test, and have reliable transportation. They must also be physically able to do the work and meet post-selection documentation and classroom requirements.
posting.contactName: Plumbers and Pipefitters Binghamton Local Union #112
posting.contactSummary: Contact Local Union #112 for more information about the apprenticeship.
